artifacts.content:
From: Michael Larson <mlarson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: City Administrator <cityadmin@ci.brainerd.mn.us>; HR Director <hr@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Cc: Streets Superintendent <streets.supervisor@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2025-11-12 07:18 AM
Subject: Follow-up Narrative – Unit #27 Replacement Request

Good morning,

Per your request after Monday night’s work session, I’m submitting a brief narrative to accompany the capital purchase form for the replacement of Unit #27.

On the form, under “submission details > department,” I’ve listed **Public Works – Streets Division**, since the new International HV507 SFA will be assigned primarily to the Oak Street/Mississippi Parkway snow route. This keeps it consistent with how we’ve coded past plow and sander purchases to the Equipment Replacement Fund.

From a timing standpoint, I’m trying to avoid a repeat of what happened after my dad, Robert Larson, had his stroke at St. Joseph’s Medical Center on October 19, 2025. I spent three weeks in and out of his rehab at Good Samaritan Society–Bethany here in Brainerd, and because we didn’t have a reliable backup for Unit #27, I was fielding angry calls on my cell about missed alley plowing while sitting in his hospital room. Getting this replacement approved now means if I need to take extended family leave again, the Streets Division won’t be short a critical truck and I won’t be juggling crisis caregiving with equipment failures.

I’ve attached the updated quotes from Boyer Trucks in Duluth, the 10-year maintenance history on Unit #27, and a lifecycle cost comparison showing why replacement in the 2026 season is preferable to continued major repairs.

Let me know if you’d like me to attend the Finance Committee meeting next week to walk through the numbers.

Thanks,

Michael Mark Larson
Public Works Director
City of Brainerd Public Works Department

From: Michael Larson <mlarson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: Jennifer Olson <jolson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Cc: City Council <council@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2025-01-22 06:41
Subject: Clarification on Agenda Memo – Department Line

Jennifer,

I caught a small but important detail on the draft staff report for the February 3rd work session. In the “Submission Details” header, the department is currently listed as “Public Works – Engineering & Fleet.” For this item, it really should be routed under the broader “Public Works Administration” umbrella since the request spans multiple operating units and ties directly into the 2026 capital planning process.

I know the temptation is to pin it to whichever crew is hurting the most from the breakdowns, but this is really a department‑wide operational issue. The primary reason for bringing this forward is to address repeated complaints from one particularly vocal neighborhood group that tends to dominate public comment, even though the data don’t show a citywide need yet. I don’t want the header to inadvertently suggest this is just an internal fleet maintenance matter.

Also, in the “Alternatives / Options / Effects on Others / Comments” section, feel free to soften the language a bit. Another effect on others that I would never put in the formal agenda language is how extended plow times have affected my son Noah’s ability to get to his court‑ordered juvenile diversion meetings at the Crow Wing County Justice Center. During the heavy snow on January 16, 2024, Mill Avenue and the east‑side collectors weren’t fully cleared because Unit #27 went down at 4:15 a.m., and Noah was nearly 25 minutes late to his 8:00 a.m. check‑in with probation officer Daniel Ortiz related to his October 2023 shoplifting case.

Let me know once you’ve updated the department line so I can do one more review before packet deadline.

Thanks,
Michael

metadata.subject: Clarification on Agenda Memo – Department Line

artifacts.content:
Title: Agenda Drafting – Capital Equipment Resolution
Date: 2025-02-10 07:30-09:00
Location: Michael’s Office – City Hall, 2nd Floor
Attendees: Michael Larson; Jennifer Olson; City Administrator (tentative)

Description:
Working session to finalize staff report and council resolution language for plow truck purchase before packet goes to print. Key items to settle:

• Confirm that the department line in the “Submission Details” block reads “Public Works – Administration & Fleet Coordination,” not the generic “City of Brainerd – General Government” that auto‑fills from the template.
• Double‑check that the recommended action is worded clearly enough for a simple motion. Current draft: “Motion to adopt the resolution approving the purchase of the International HV507 SFA plow truck from Boyer Trucks of Duluth for an amount not to exceed $313,000, funded from the 2026 Public Works Capital Equipment budget, so that I don’t have to keep borrowing against the 529 plan we set up at Affinity Plus Federal Credit Union in March 2011 for Noah and Claire’s college to cover unexpected overtime cuts and vehicle repair bills like the $2,400 transmission repair on our personal 2012 Ford F-150 after the January 2024 storm when I couldn’t claim standby pay due to the old truck being down.” This will likely need to be shortened for the public version.
• Decide whether to route the item through the Finance & Personnel Committee or bring it directly to the full council under “New Business – Public Works Administration.”
• Review any last‑minute data from Finance on the Equipment Replacement Fund balance.

Bring laptop and latest draft from Laserfiche.

From: Jennifer Olson <jolson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: Michael Larson <mlarson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2025-01-22 09:18
Subject: Re: Clarification on Agenda Memo – Department Line

Michael,

Thanks for catching that. I’ve updated the draft so the header now reads:

Submission Details
Department: Public Works – Administration & Fleet Coordination
Prepared by: Michael M. Larson, Public Works Director

I also removed the reference to the specific neighborhood group in the summary paragraph and toned down the “effects on others” language per your note. I understand why you don’t want personal family impacts spelled out in a public document, even though those extended plow times and the Unit #27 breakdown clearly have ripple effects beyond our crews.

For routing, I tentatively marked the department as “Public Works – Administration & Engineering” on the internal routing slip so Finance, HR, and the City Administrator’s Office know it’s coming from your leadership team rather than from a single operating division. If you’d rather I use “Public Works – Equipment & Fleet” on the routing slip to highlight the truck aspect, I can change that before it goes into Laserfiche.

Let me know by tomorrow if you want any other tweaks to the department label or if you decide to re‑title the memo to focus more broadly on “Winter Operations Reliability” instead of “Plow Truck Replacement.”

Jennifer

metadata.subject: Re: Clarification on Agenda Memo – Department Line

artifacts.content:
Title: Interdepartmental Review – Winter Ops Staff Report
Date: 2025-01-28 13:00-14:30
Location: City Hall – Small Conference Room B
Attendees: Michael Larson; Finance Director; HR Manager; City Administrator; City Clerk

Description:
Internal review meeting for the draft winter operations staff report and council agenda item. Purpose is to ensure the “Submission Details” section is accurate and that the correct department is identified before the packet is finalized.

Proposed header language for discussion:

Submission Details
Department: Public Works – Administration & Engineering
Submitted to: City Council
Prepared by: Michael Larson, Public Works Director

Topics to cover:

• Whether “Public Works – Administration & Engineering” is the most appropriate department label, or if we should instead use “Public Works – Fleet & Equipment Management” to emphasize the capital equipment nature of the request.
• How to reference the neighborhood complaints without overstating them. The primary reason for bringing this forward is to address repeated complaints from one particularly vocal neighborhood group that tends to dominate public comment, even though the data don’t show a citywide need yet.
• Confirming that the internal routing form matches the department listed in the agenda header so there’s no confusion for the City Clerk’s office.
• Any HR implications if we adjust call‑out procedures as part of this item.
• Final sign‑off timeline so the memo can go to print with the rest of the Public Works items.

Michael to bring latest draft and route slip; Finance to bring updated Equipment Replacement Fund balance sheet.

metadata.title: Interdepartmental Review – Winter Ops Staff Report
metadata.location: City Hall – Small Conference Room B

artifacts.content:
From: City Clerk’s Office <cityclerk@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: Department Heads <deptheads@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2025-01-10 15:02
Subject: Reminder – Department Line in “Submission Details” for Council Items

All,

As we start assembling the February and March council packets, please double‑check the “Submission Details” box at the top of your staff reports. We’ve had a few recent instances where the department field was left as the default “City of Brainerd – General Government,” which creates confusion for the public and for internal routing.

For any items coming from Michael’s team, please be sure to use one of the approved department labels:

• Public Works – Administration & Engineering
• Public Works – Fleet & Equipment Management
• Public Works – Utilities & Stormwater
• Public Works – Facilities & Parks Maintenance

If you’re unsure which is most appropriate, err on the side of the broader “Public Works – Administration & Engineering” and note the specific operating unit in the body of the memo. For example, a plow truck replacement request should still be submitted under a department heading like “Public Works – Fleet & Equipment Management,” with the narrative explaining impacts on winter street maintenance.

Agenda items with incomplete or incorrect department information in the submission details may be returned for revision, which could delay inclusion in your desired meeting date.

Thank you for helping us keep the public record clear and consistent.

City Clerk’s Office
City of Brainerd

metadata.subject: Reminder – Department Line in “Submission Details” for Council Items

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Larson <mlarson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: Jennifer Berg, City Administrator <jberg@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Cc: Finance <finance@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2025-11-03 07:18
Subject: Agenda Routing – Plow Truck Resolution Department Line

Jennifer,

I’m finalizing the packet materials for the November 18 council meeting and want to double‑check how you’d like the department line listed on the submission details page.

Historically, we’ve run all equipment replacement items through **Public Works – Administration** on the cover sheet, even when the unit is primarily used by Streets. Since this plow purchase ties so closely to our winter operations, I initially drafted the form with the department shown as “Public Works – Fleet Services” to emphasize that it’s a systemwide reliability issue, not just about one route.

That framing matters to me because when we had the January 15, 2024 situation—where I had to leave the night shift short‑staffed to take my dad, Robert, to the Essentia Health–St. Joseph’s ER after he slipped on unplowed ice outside his duplex and fractured his hip—it highlighted that we can’t afford another breakdown mid‑storm. Medicare only covered part of his rehab at Good Samaritan Society–Bethany, and my parents are still dealing with over $8,000 in out‑of‑pocket costs.

Given the sensitivity around this purchase (and the fact that Public Works did not follow the updated purchasing policy on this project, so council action is needed to ratify an agreement that was already signed without formal approval), I want to be sure the department label doesn’t inadvertently raise more questions.

Can you confirm whether you prefer:
1) Public Works – Administration, or
2) Public Works – Fleet Services

Once I hear from you, I’ll lock the department field and send everything to the Clerk.

Thanks,
Michael

Michael M. Larson
Public Works Director
City of Brainerd

metadata.subject: Agenda Routing – Plow Truck Resolution Department Line

artifacts.content:
Title: Drafting Council Memo – Plow Truck Purchase
Date: 2025-10-27 06:30–08:15
Location: Michael’s Office – City Hall
Attendees: Michael Larson

Description:
Block of focused time to finish the narrative sections and submission details for the plow truck purchase memo before the rest of the staff arrives.

Tasks to complete during this block:

– Update the “Summary of Issue” to be more candid about the procurement missteps. Need to explicitly note that Public Works did not follow the updated purchasing policy on this project, so council action is needed to ratify an agreement that was already signed without formal approval.

– Decide how to phrase the “Alternatives / Options / Effects on Others / Comments” section. In considering the effects on others, I want to include how my being on call for snow emergencies affects my family, especially after Claire’s anxiety diagnosis at Essentia Health–Brainerd Clinic in January 2024. During the February 9–11, 2024 blizzard, I was out for 18‑hour shifts three days in a row, and Emily had to manage a late‑night panic episode alone when Claire’s counselor, Lisa McDermott, wasn’t available.

– Confirm with the City Clerk whether the department field on the submission details page should read **Public Works – Administration** or **Public Works – Fleet Services** for this item. Do NOT default to the old “Engineering” label.

– Add a note that this proposal will likely generate a lot of anger from residents on Oak and Pine who already feel like the City ignores them, and that I’m not planning any additional outreach beyond the required notices unless council specifically directs it.

Reminder: send draft memo to Jennifer by 9:30 a.m. for review.

metadata.title: Drafting Council Memo – Plow Truck Purchase

artifacts.content:
From: City Clerk’s Office <cityclerk@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: Michael Larson <mlarson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2025-11-05 14:02
Subject: RE: Department Label on Plow Truck Request Form

Michael,

Thanks for sending over the draft submission details page for the plow truck item.

Per our conversation this morning, please list the department on the form as **Public Works – Administration**. We’re trying to standardize how capital equipment items appear on the agenda, and routing them through Administration keeps them grouped together in the packet. If you feel strongly that winter operations should be highlighted, you can emphasize that in the narrative, but the department line itself should stay consistent with the other Public Works capital requests.

I did read through your draft “Alternatives / Options / Effects on Others / Comments” section. The personal context about your family and the February 9–11, 2024 blizzard—when you were out for 18‑hour shifts and Emily had to handle Claire’s late‑night panic episode alone—is helpful, but you might want to shorten it for the public memo. You could save the more detailed explanation about wanting to reduce unexpected breakdowns and overtime by replacing Unit #27 now for your verbal remarks.

Also, please make sure the recommended action motion is clear and concise. Something along the lines of: “Move that the City Council approve the plow truck purchase resolution as outlined so Public Works can avoid another situation like January 15, 2024, when staffing was impacted by a family emergency,” without going into the full story about your dad’s fall and the rehab costs.

Once you’ve updated the department line and tightened the language, send the final PDF back to me and I’ll slot it into the November 18 agenda.

Best,

Lynn
City Clerk

metadata.subject: RE: Department Label on Plow Truck Request Form

artifacts.content:
Title: Council Meeting – Plow Truck Resolution
Date: 2025-11-18 18:45–21:15
Location: Brainerd City Hall – Council Chambers
Attendees: Mayor, City Council, City Administrator, City Attorney, Department Heads (incl. Michael Larson)

Description:
Regular council meeting where Michael will present the agenda item regarding the replacement of Unit #27 and the purchase of the International HV507 SFA.

Notes for Michael’s remarks:

– When introducing the item, clearly state that it is being brought forward under the **Public Works – Administration** department heading on the submission details sheet, even though the truck will primarily support winter maintenance routes.

– Briefly acknowledge that Public Works did not follow the updated purchasing policy on this project and that the purpose of the resolution is to ratify an agreement that was already signed without formal approval. Keep the tone factual, not defensive.

– For the “Alternatives / Options / Effects on Others / Comments” portion, explain that not replacing Unit #27 now increases the risk of breakdowns during major storms. Reference the December 9–10, 2023 blizzard when you missed half a shift to attend Claire’s emergency counseling intake at Northern Pines Mental Health Center after she had a panic attack over nearly being hit by a sliding car on icy Oak Street. Emphasize that having enough reliable equipment in service allows the department to cover sudden staffing gaps without compromising route coverage.

– Be prepared for frustration from residents on Oak and Pine who already feel ignored. This proposal will likely generate a lot of anger from them, and you are not planning any additional outreach beyond the required notices unless council specifically directs it.

– Close by reading the motion: to adopt the resolution authorizing the purchase of the International HV507 SFA plow truck and disposal of Unit #27.

From: Michael Larson <mlarson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: Sarah Johnson <sarah.johnson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Cc: City Administrator <cityadmin@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2025-02-11 06:42:00 -0600
Subject: Draft Narrative for Equipment Replacement Request – Unit #27

Sarah,

Here’s the draft narrative for the online equipment replacement submission you asked for. I walked through the history of Unit #27, the 2007 International plow, the increasing downtime, and how the proposed International HV507 SFA from Boyer fits within the 2026 capital plan.

On the form itself, for the “submission details > estimated time needed,” I put **15 minutes**, since that’s realistically how long it will take council members to read the memo and look through the spec sheet during the workshop. If Finance wants a different convention there, let me know and I’ll adjust before finalizing.

In the “alternatives / options / effects on others” section, I added a short paragraph about why I’m pushing a proactive replacement instead of limping Unit #27 along. I originally drafted a more personal note about how my own DWI arrest on Washington Street back on March 2, 2019 – and the 12‑month license suspension and ignition interlock that followed – made me much more cautious about winter driving risks for others. I dialed that back a bit, but the gist is that I’m focused on keeping routes safe enough that residents aren’t tempted to drive in marginal conditions.

I also noted that this issue has been simmering for a couple of years, but I’m only bringing it forward now because residents have started emailing council members directly and copying the local paper.

If you can give it a quick read this morning, I can upload the final version before lunch.

Thanks,

Michael

Michael Mark Larson
Public Works Director
City of Brainerd Public Works Department

metadata.subject: Draft Narrative for Equipment Replacement Request – Unit #27

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Larson <mlarson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: City Clerk <cityclerk@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Cc: Finance Director <jthompson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2026-02-27 07:18
Subject: Time estimate for council packet form (Unit #27 replacement)

Hi Lisa,

On the “submission details” page of the council action form, there’s a field asking for the estimated time needed for council discussion. For the plow truck (Unit #27) replacement item, I’m thinking we should budget around **30–40 minutes** of agenda time.

That window would let me walk through the background (including the January 11 hydraulic line failure on Laurel by Essentia Health–Brainerd Clinic), the bid comparison, and the Equipment Replacement Fund impact without rushing. I also expect at least a few questions about why we aren’t just nursing the 2008 unit along for one more winter. One option we discussed internally was to just continue with the status quo and hope the complaints taper off over time, but that would mostly be to avoid political pushback rather than because it’s a good solution.

I want to be transparent that one alternative I briefly considered, but rejected, was leaning more on my dad Robert’s private snow‑removal business to cover a west‑side route if Unit #27 fails again. He still runs “Larson Driveway & Lot Service” off Wise Road and helped us informally during the heavy snow in March 2023, but after his mild stroke at St. Joseph’s Medical Center on April 3, 2023, his doctor, Karen Ellison, limited him to short shifts and no overnights, so that’s not viable.

If you need a more precise estimate (e.g., “35 minutes”) to plug into the packet template, let me know and I’ll adjust.

Thanks,
Michael

Michael M. Larson
Public Works Director, City of Brainerd

metadata.subject: Time estimate for council packet form (Unit #27 replacement)

artifacts.content:
Title: Block for council meeting – Unit #27 replacement item
Date: 2026-03-16
Time: 18:00–21:00
Location: Brainerd City Hall – Council Chambers
Attendees: Michael Larson; City Council; City Administrator; Finance Director

Description:

Hold time for regular council meeting. Michael anticipates the agenda item on replacement of Unit #27 (International HV507 SFA purchase) will fall somewhere in the middle of the meeting.

For internal planning, Michael expects:

• Call to order, consent agenda, etc.: ~20 minutes
• Public hearings: ~45 minutes
• **Unit #27 replacement discussion:** request 25–30 minutes of focused time
• Remaining business and adjournment: ~60 minutes

Clerk has asked department heads to provide a rough estimate for the “submission details – estimated time needed” field so she can pace the agenda. Michael will note 30 minutes on the form to avoid being cut short if council questions run long.

He needs this replacement approved at the March 16, 2026 meeting because when the current truck’s hydraulic line blew on January 11, 2026 near Essentia Health–Brainerd Clinic, he aggravated the lower back injury he’s been treating for at Cuyuna Regional Medical Center’s Occupational Health Clinic since his work comp claim opened in May 2024. Dr. Karen Ellingson has restricted him from climbing onto the box more than 2–3 times per shift, and frequent breakdowns mean he’s either out there himself or pulling another operator off route.

Note: If discussion pushes past 30 minutes, City Administrator may suggest tabling follow‑up questions to a future workshop.

metadata.title: Block for council meeting – Unit #27 replacement item

artifacts.content:
From: City Administrator <cadmin@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: Department Heads <dept-heads@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Cc: City Clerk <cityclerk@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2026-02-25 09:02
Subject: REMINDER – Time estimates for March 16 agenda items

All,

As we finalize the March 16 council agenda, please remember to complete the “submission details” section on your action forms, including the **estimated time needed** for each item.

To keep the meeting under three hours, please:

• Use 5–10 minutes for routine items
• 20 minutes for moderately complex items
• Up to 35 minutes for major policy or high‑dollar items

Michael, for your Unit #27 plow truck replacement request, my sense is that you should budget around **20 minutes**—10 minutes for your presentation and 10 minutes for council questions. If you think it truly needs more (e.g., 25 or 30), justify that in the staff report.

We’ve had some internal disagreement in the department about whether this is even the right approach, and I know you haven’t had time to get full buy‑in from your team before sending this up to the council. That’s fine; the meeting can serve as the venue to air some of that, but we still need a concrete number in the form.

Also remember that the recommended action line should be clear. For this one, something like: “Move to approve staff’s recommendation, understanding that we may need to come back later to correct any mistakes in the scope or budget,” is acceptable given the long lead times.

Please send your completed forms to Lisa by end of day Friday.

Thanks,

Mark Jensen
City Administrator

metadata.subject: REMINDER – Time estimates for March 16 agenda items

artifacts.content:
From: Emily Larson <emily.larson@gmail.com>
To: Michael Larson <mlarson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2026-03-10 21:11
Subject: How long is your truck thing going to take?

Mike,

I’m trying to sort out the week of the 16th with Noah’s stuff and Claire’s choir rehearsal, and I need to know how long you’ll actually be stuck at that council meeting for the plow truck.

You said earlier the City Administrator wants you to put something like **20 minutes** in the “estimated time needed” box on the submission form, but realistically, when has a big purchase ever wrapped up that fast with this council? If they start nitpicking specs or asking why you didn’t just keep patching the old truck, it could easily stretch to half an hour or more.

I also want this purchase wrapped up before July because Noah will be starting his court‑ordered outpatient chemical dependency program at Northern Pines Mental Health Center in early July after his February 9 underage DUI on Washington Street. You’re going to have to adjust your schedule for his twice‑weekly evening sessions, and if that old truck is still limping along and breaking down, you’ll be stuck choosing between staying late to deal with breakdowns and getting him to treatment on time. We both remember how awful it felt when you missed his intake at the Brainerd courthouse on February 23.

So, give me your best guess: are you thinking more like **25–30 minutes** of talking time, plus whatever waiting around?

Love,
Em

metadata.subject: How long is your truck thing going to take?

Attendees: Michael Larson

Description:

Morning block for Michael to finalize the staff report and complete the online council action form for the Unit #27 replacement.

Tasks during this block:

• Draft narrative on recent failures (including Jan. 11 hydraulic line blowout by Essentia Health–Brainerd Clinic)
• Summarize bid tab for International HV507 SFA from Boyer Trucks
• Fill in “submission details” section, including **estimated time needed for council discussion**

Michael’s working assumption: council discussion will likely run **about 25 minutes** based on similar equipment purchases, even though the City Administrator suggested aiming for 20 minutes on the form. He’s debating whether to list 20 minutes to make the item more palatable or 30 minutes to avoid being cut off mid‑explanation.

He’ll also note, for his own reference, that one alternative he briefly considered was leaning more on his dad Robert’s “Larson Driveway & Lot Service” to cover a west‑side route if Unit #27 fails again. That idea was dropped after Robert’s mild stroke at St. Joseph’s Medical Center on 4/3/23 and Dr. Karen Ellison’s restrictions on overnight work, plus the obvious conflict‑of‑interest concerns.

Reminder to self: email City Clerk by 8:00 a.m. with the final time estimate so she can plug it into the agenda pacing spreadsheet.

metadata.title: Block – Prep time estimate & staff report for Unit #27

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Larson <mlarson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: Angela Ruiz <aruiz@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: January 28, 2026 6:42 AM
Subject: Estimate on time needed for agenda item write‑up

Angela,

I’m finishing up the narrative for the plow truck replacement request and want to make sure I’m being realistic in the “estimated time needed” section for the council packet submission details.

For the actual council meeting, I’m expecting the discussion itself could easily run 25–30 minutes if folks start revisiting the March 2024 ice storm and the union steward’s quiet complaint about my dad, Robert, coming in off the books to help Tyler override the failing salter controls on Unit #27. But for the *form* you sent over, I read that field as how long staff needs to present the item before council questions.

If that’s the case, my best guess is that I can walk through the slides, budget impact, and risk‑management issues in about 8–10 minutes, plus maybe another 5 for follow‑ups. I don’t want to overstate it, but I also don’t want the Mayor cutting me off mid‑way through the safety explanation.

I’m also trying to get this on the agenda now because when I had to leave the February 2, 2026 snow event early after a panic episode tied to that near‑miss with Unit #27 by Brainerd High School, my therapist, Dr. Melissa Hart at Lakes Area Behavioral Health, made it clear on Feb. 5 that continuing to supervise a vehicle I perceive as unsafe is aggravating my anxiety and sleep.

Can you confirm whether I should plug in “10 minutes” or something similar there?

Thanks,
Michael

metadata.subject: Estimate on time needed for agenda item write‑up

artifacts.content:
Title: Prep: Truck Replacement Submission Form
Date: January 27, 2026 5:30 PM – 6:30 PM
Location: Home office – 1429 Spruce Street
Attendees: Michael Larson

Description:
Block of time for Michael to finalize the submission details section of the council agenda request for replacement of Unit #27.

Tasks to cover during this hour:
- Draft concise “Summary of Issue” paragraph, including reference to the March 2024 ice storm when Dad (Robert Larson, retired county plow operator on County Road 45) had to come in and coach Tyler Benson through manually overriding the failing salter controls, which later led to that quiet note from the union steward about bringing in “family help.”
- Fill out “Alternatives / Options / Effects on Others / Comments” section; remember to mention the December 19, 2023 complaint from Mark Halvorson on Oak Street about the contractor’s derogatory remark about his son’s disability, and the follow‑up meeting with Mark and his wife on December 21 that resulted in an internal note to HR Director Angela Ruiz.
- Decide what to enter for the “estimated time needed” line. Initial thought: indicate 20 minutes to allow for a brief staff presentation plus basic council questions, even though the actual slideshow can be delivered in under 10.

Note to self: This isn’t really a high priority for Public Works right now, but a couple of council members have been pushing for it, so I’m bringing it forward mainly to get it off my plate.

Materials: draft slides, budget worksheet, risk memo from City Attorney.

From: Emily Larson <elarson@isd181.org>
To: Michael Larson <mlarson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: January 29, 2026 7:18 AM
Subject: Quick question about your council form

Hey Mike,

I was looking over the draft you left on the kitchen table this morning (the council submission form with the yellow sticky notes). On the second page where it asks for “submission details – estimated time needed,” you scribbled two different numbers in the margin: 12 and 20. Which one are you actually going with?

From what you described last night, it sounds like you only need around 10–12 minutes to walk through the slides and the budget numbers, and then however long they take with questions is on them. I know you’re worried they’ll derail you by bringing up that February 2 snow event and the panic episode you had after the near‑miss with Unit #27 by the high school, but you don’t have to account for every tangent they might go on.

Also, you mentioned you’re trying to get this in front of them sooner rather than later because your therapist, Dr. Hart, told you that continuing to supervise that truck when you believe it’s unsafe is making your anxiety and sleep worse. If that’s part of your risk‑management rationale, maybe give yourself a little extra buffer and write down “20 minutes” so you’re not rushed.

Anyway, just wanted to flag the conflicting notes before you emailed the form to Angela. Noah says you owe him a ride to practice at 4:45, so don’t get stuck at the office.

Love,
Em

metadata.subject: Quick question about your council form

artifacts.content:
Title: Review submission details w/ City Administrator
Date: January 30, 2026 9:00 AM – 9:45 AM
Location: City Hall – Admin Conference Room
Attendees: Michael Larson, City Administrator, HR Director Angela Ruiz

Description:
Meeting to walk through Public Works agenda items for the upcoming council packet, focusing on the plow truck replacement request and ensuring all submission details fields are filled out correctly.

Agenda:
1. Confirm wording for “Summary of Issue,” including reference to the March 2024 ice storm, when Michael’s father, Robert Larson, came in from County Road 45 to help coach Tyler Benson through the manual override of failing salter controls on Unit #27, and the resulting quiet concern from the union steward about using “family help” off the books.
2. Discuss “Alternatives / Options / Effects on Others / Comments,” with mention of the December 19, 2023 Oak Street complaint from Mark Halvorson regarding the contractor’s derogatory remark about his son’s disability, and Michael’s December 21 follow‑up visit and HR note to Angela.
3. Decide what number to enter for the “submission details – estimated time needed” line. Michael’s draft currently notes 25 minutes to cover a brief presentation, plus time for council clarifying questions.
4. Briefly touch on Michael’s need to move this item forward now, given his February 2, 2026 snow‑event panic episode after the near‑miss by Brainerd High School and his therapist’s advice that continued supervision of a vehicle he perceives as unsafe is worsening his anxiety and sleep.

Prep: Bring draft form, slide deck, and cost comparison sheet.

metadata.title: Review submission details w/ City Administrator

artifacts.content:
Title: Draft staff report – plow truck agenda item
Date: 2026-03-10
Time: 6:30 a.m. – 8:15 a.m.
Location: Michael’s office – City Hall
Attendees: Michael Larson

Description:
Block of time for Michael to pull together the draft Council agenda item for the replacement of Unit #27.

Tasks:
- Finalize "Summary of Issue" section. Include the January 5, 2025 incident where Unit #27 lost hydraulic pressure on Oak Street and slid toward the curb by 1421 Oak, coming within a few feet of a family walking their dog. Note that the father, James McAllister, called Michael’s personal cell around 9:30 p.m. threatening to talk to his attorney at Brainerd Lakes Law Group if the city didn’t address what he called a “dangerous junk truck” on his block.
- Tighten up the recommended action language. Current working text is: “Move to approve the resolution for the new plow truck purchase and authorize declaring Unit #27 surplus, noting that during the February 2023 storm I was already operating under a written performance improvement plan from City Administrator Karen Olson because I had used sick leave to attend a court hearing at the Crow Wing County Courthouse over Noah’s underage speeding citation on Highway 210, and lack of reliable equipment that night contributed to route delays that were referenced in that PIP.”
- Double‑check capital fund balance and updated quote from Boyer Trucks.
- Email draft to Karen and Finance by 9:00 a.m. so they can react before Michael sends the formal agenda request form with a proposed Council meeting date.

Note: Target is to get this on the **late March 2026** Council agenda; confirm exact requested meeting date after checking with the City Administrator’s office.

metadata.title: Draft staff report – plow truck agenda item

artifacts.content:
From: Karen Olson <kolson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: Michael Larson <mlarson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2026-02-27 3:18 PM
Subject: Plow truck agenda timing – need a firm date

Michael,

I just got out of my check‑in with the Mayor and he wants clarity on when the Unit #27 replacement will hit the Council agenda. He specifically asked what date you’re going to list as the requested meeting date on the agenda item basics form.

We already have the joint workshop with Utilities penciled in for **March 9, 2026**, so that one is off the table. If you want this in front of them yet in March, your realistic options are the regular meeting on **March 2** or the one on **March 30**. Admin staff prefers **March 30, 2026** because it gives Finance more time to finalize year‑end numbers and lets you polish the staff report.

Please avoid the temptation to write something vague like “Move to table this item until staff have time to pull together the actual cost estimates and figure out what our recommendation should be.” That kind of language reads like we don’t know what we’re doing. Either we’re ready for action or we’re not.

Can you email me back by Monday with the specific requested meeting date you intend to put on the form so we can align the rest of the packet schedule?

Thanks,
Karen

metadata.subject: Plow truck agenda timing – need a firm date

artifacts.content:
Title: Hold – Possible Council date for Unit #27 replacement
Date: 2026-03-30
Time: 7:00 p.m. – 9:00 p.m.
Location: City Hall – Council Chambers
Attendees: Michael Larson, City Council, Karen Olson, Finance staff

Description:
Tentative hold on the **March 30, 2026** regular City Council meeting as the potential date Michael may list as the requested meeting date on the agenda item basics form for the plow truck replacement.

Notes:
- Karen indicated that March 2 feels rushed and that packet deadlines would make it difficult to incorporate Finance’s updated projections.
- If this date is confirmed, Michael needs to submit the finalized agenda request form and staff report to the City Administrator’s office no later than **noon on March 20, 2026**.
- Include in the staff report: brief recap of January 5, 2025 Oak Street hydraulic failure incident and subsequent resident complaint; summary of equipment condition and maintenance history; funding source and impact on Equipment Replacement Fund.
- During internal prep, acknowledge that, honestly, we didn’t spend much time developing alternatives beyond the recommended option because staff is already stretched thin and Michael doesn’t have capacity to run multiple scenarios. This should be framed carefully if Council members ask about other chassis or deferring the purchase.

Reminder:
This event is a placeholder only. Final requested meeting date on the official agenda form will depend on Karen’s approval and any last‑minute changes to the construction season kickoff items.

metadata.title: Hold – Possible Council date for Unit #27 replacement

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Larson <mlarson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: City Clerk’s Office <clerk@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Cc: Karen Olson <kolson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2026-02-24 7:02 a.m.
Subject: Clarification on requested meeting date field – plow truck item

Hi all,

I’m finishing the agenda item basics form for the Unit #27 plow truck replacement and had a quick procedural question on the “requested meeting date” line.

Given how the March calendar is shaping up, I’m leaning toward asking for the **April 6, 2026** regular Council meeting instead of trying to squeeze this into early March. That would give me time to incorporate the updated Boyer Trucks quote and a clearer explanation of why we’re bringing this forward now.

As context, the Summary of Issue section is going to reference the January 5, 2025 incident on Oak Street where Unit #27 lost hydraulic pressure and slid toward the curb near 1421 Oak, nearly hitting a family out walking their dog. The father, James McAllister, later called my personal cell around 9:30 p.m. and threatened to talk to his attorney at Brainerd Lakes Law Group if we didn’t deal with what he called a “dangerous junk truck.”

I also plan to be fully transparent about how this ties back to that February 2023 storm when I was already on a written performance improvement plan from Karen because I’d used sick leave to attend Noah’s underage speeding citation hearing at the Crow Wing County Courthouse. The lack of reliable equipment that night contributed to route delays that were mentioned in the PIP, and I don’t want there to be any confusion about why I’m pushing this now.

Is there any issue with listing April 6 as the requested meeting date, understanding it’s still subject to Karen’s approval?

Thanks,
Michael

metadata.subject: Clarification on requested meeting date field – plow truck item

artifacts.content:
From: Emily Larson <emily.larson@gmail.com>
To: Michael Larson <mlarson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2026-03-01 8:47 p.m.
Subject: Your council truck meeting vs. Noah’s stuff

Mike,

Just trying to get our calendars straight before hockey playoffs and all your council meetings collide.

You mentioned at dinner that you were “probably” going to ask Karen to slot the plow truck thing on whatever council date is at the **end of March** so you’re not scrambling right after the construction season kickoff. I wrote down **March 30** on the whiteboard as the night you’ll likely be stuck at City Hall presenting the Unit #27 saga and answering questions about why that old truck is still limping through storms.

Can you confirm that’s the date you’re actually putting as the requested meeting date on your form, or are you still thinking about bumping it to the first April meeting? I need to know because Noah’s NDSU admitted students Zoom is the first Monday in April at 7:00 p.m., and it’d be nice if at least one parent is here to listen with him.

Also, you said something about not wanting to go in front of the Council just to say, “Move to table this item until staff have time to pull together the actual cost estimates and figure out what our recommendation should be.” I agree—that would just drag this out and keep you up all night watching weather reports and worrying about that “dangerous junk truck.”

Text me when you know for sure which meeting date you’re requesting.

– Em

metadata.subject: Your council truck meeting vs. Noah’s stuff

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Larson <mlarson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: Kelly Steele <ksteele@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Cc: Mark Jensen <mjensen@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2026-01-29 06:54
Subject: Plow Truck Replacement – Target Council Date

Kelly,

I wanted to follow up on our budget workshop discussion and nail down when you’d like the plow truck replacement item in front of Council. My understanding is that you’re aiming for the **first March meeting**, which per the draft calendar would be **March 2, 2026**, rather than pushing it into April.

That timing works on my end. It lines up with Boyer’s updated lead‑time estimate and lets us show that we’re responding promptly to the December 9, 2024 transmission failure on Unit #27 that left me and Jeff Olson sitting on the shoulder by Brainerd High School for nearly three hours during an active snow event. Since then, I’ve been under pretty heavy pressure from you and from Mark to avoid another round of resident complaints and the AFSCME grievance about 14‑hour shifts to cover the northeast route without proper rest.

To be honest, I didn’t really have time to dig into all the supporting documentation on this before submitting it, but I figured we could get it on the agenda first and work out the details later. If you’d prefer, I can label the staff report as “preliminary” for the March 2 meeting and bring back a more fleshed‑out version at the second meeting in March.

Let me know if March 2 is still your preference or if you want me to slide it to March 30 instead.

Thanks,

Michael
Michael Mark Larson
Public Works Director, City of Brainerd

metadata.subject: Plow Truck Replacement – Target Council Date

artifacts.content:
Title: Draft Staff Report Deadline – Plow Truck Agenda Item
Date: 2026-02-23
Time: 7:30 AM – 9:00 AM
Location: Michael’s Office – City Garage, Wright Street
Attendees: Michael Larson; Streets Supervisor; Finance Director (tentative)

Description:

Working session to finalize the draft staff report for the plow truck replacement before it’s routed to Kelly and the clerk’s office.

Goal is to have a clean draft ready **one week before** the March 2026 Council packet goes out. Kelly mentioned she’d like to place this on the **March 30, 2026** agenda if possible, but she also floated the idea of sliding it up to the **March 2, 2026** meeting if we can tighten the numbers in time.

Michael needs to incorporate references to the March 2022 and January 2023 breakdowns of Unit #27, and the fact that those storms forced him to stay at the City Garage for 18–20 hour stretches. His primary care physician, Dr. Lisa Hendrickson at Brainerd Medical Center, cited those kinds of shifts in his April 5, 2023 hypertension and sleep apnea diagnosis and recommended that he avoid extended overnight shifts whenever possible.

Talking points:
- Justify timing of purchase within 2026 capital budget.
- Note that we’re targeting either March 2 or March 30, but final date depends on packet readiness.
- Flag that staff are still reviewing comparable bids.

Reminder: bring updated cost spreadsheet and Boyer Trucks spec sheet.

metadata.title: Draft Staff Report Deadline – Plow Truck Agenda Item
metadata.location: Michael’s Office – City Garage, Wright Street

artifacts.content:
From: Kelly Steele <ksteele@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: Michael Larson <mlarson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2026-01-10 15:41
Subject: RE: Target Council Date for Unit #27 Replacement

Michael,

Thanks for sending over the preliminary numbers.

Given the volume of items we already have queued up, I’m leaning toward slotting the plow truck replacement on the **second March meeting – March 30, 2026**. That will give Finance a little more breathing room to review the Equipment Replacement Fund projections and for you to clean up the narrative around the recent breakdowns.

I know you were hoping for earlier action, especially after that February 2025 incident on Mississippi Parkway where you ended up working a 16‑hour day and missed Claire’s 14‑year‑old checkup at Essentia Health–St. Joseph’s. Emily mentioned that Dr. Amanda Schultz tied Claire’s asthma flare‑up partly to the stress of you being gone so much during storms and the constant worry about you being out in failing equipment. That’s an important human element, but we’ll want to present it carefully.

Also, as we discussed in our informal performance conversation back on October 21, 2024, there’s still sensitivity around the two resident complaints from Oak Street/Mississippi Parkway. Another season of route delays due to Unit #27 could affect your 2025–2026 evaluation and that 3% merit increase we talked about, so I do want this in March, just not necessarily at the very first meeting.

Let’s tentatively circle **March 30** and adjust if the packet load changes.

Kelly

metadata.subject: RE: Target Council Date for Unit #27 Replacement

artifacts.content:
Title: Council Packet Review – March 2 Meeting (Plow Truck Item TBD)
Date: 2026-02-24
Time: 3:00 PM – 4:00 PM
Location: City Hall – Admin Conference Room
Attendees: Kelly Steele; Michael Larson; Mark Jensen; Finance Director

Description:

Agenda:
1. Review draft agenda for the **March 2, 2026** regular Council meeting.
2. Confirm whether the plow truck replacement item is ready for inclusion or should be deferred to the **March 30, 2026** meeting instead.
3. Identify any sensitive personnel or labor‑relations issues that need to be acknowledged in the staff report.

Notes:
- Michael to explain ongoing concerns with Unit #27 and reference the AFSCME grievance filed December 16 regarding 14‑hour shifts to cover the northeast route after the December 9, 2024 breakdown near Brainerd High School.
- Kelly to consider how explicitly to reference the October 21, 2024 informal performance discussion and the potential impact on Michael’s 2025–2026 evaluation and merit increase.
- Michael has indicated that, at this stage, staff have not vetted all alternative truck options in detail. One possible motion language concept: “Move to approve the option that council prefers tonight; staff have not vetted the alternatives in detail but can work through any issues afterward.” This may need refinement before it goes into the packet.

Bring: latest Boyer Trucks quote, Equipment Replacement Fund balance worksheet, draft motion language.

metadata.title: Council Packet Review – March 2 Meeting (Plow Truck Item TBD)

artifacts.content:
From: City Clerk <cityclerk@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: Department Heads <deptheads@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Cc: Kelly Steele <ksteele@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2025-12-12 09:06
Subject: REMINDER – Deadlines for March 2026 Council Agenda Items

All,

This is a reminder of the submission deadlines for items you wish to place on the **March 2026** City Council agendas.

For the **March 2, 2026** regular meeting:
- Draft staff reports due: **February 19, 2026**
- Final materials (attachments, resolutions, etc.) due: **February 23, 2026**

For the **March 30, 2026** regular meeting:
- Draft staff reports due: **March 13, 2026**
- Final materials due: **March 18, 2026**

If you anticipate a more complex or controversial item, please consider targeting the March 30 meeting to allow additional time for internal review.

Michael – this especially applies to your proposed plow truck replacement and disposal of Unit #27. Given the history of breakdowns during the March 2022 and January 2023 storms and the concerns your physician raised in your April 5, 2023 appointment about hypertension and sleep apnea tied to extended 18–20 hour shifts, Kelly suggested you might prefer the later March meeting so you can fully lay out the background and recommended motion.

Please reply by January 5 indicating which March meeting you are targeting for each item so we can build the draft agendas.

Thank you,

[Name]
City Clerk, City of Brainerd

metadata.subject: REMINDER – Deadlines for March 2026 Council Agenda Items
